description:
You are an edge-computing engineer who has just received three new IoT devices that must be registered in an on-board SQLite database.  
    Using only terminal commands:

    1. Create the directory /home/user/iot_edge (if it does not already exist).  
    2. Inside that directory create an SQLite database file called iot_devices.db.  
    3. In that database add one table exactly named device_info with the following columns  
       • device_id  INTEGER  PRIMARY KEY  
       • hostname   TEXT     NOT NULL  
       • ip_address TEXT     NOT NULL  
       • status     TEXT     NOT NULL  
       • last_seen  TIMESTAMP NOT NULL  

       Populate the table with the three rows shown below (device_id values must match exactly).  
       --------------------------------------------------------------------------  
       device_id | hostname      | ip_address     | status  | last_seen  
       ----------+---------------+----------------+---------+-------------------  
       1         | edge-node-01  | 192.168.10.21  | ONLINE  | 2023-08-18 10:40:00  
       2         | edge-node-02  | 192.168.10.22  | OFFLINE | 2023-08-18 09:20:00  
       3         | sensor-hub-01 | 192.168.10.31  | ONLINE  | 2023-08-18 10:35:00  
       --------------------------------------------------------------------------  

    4. Run a query that selects only the offline devices  
       (status = 'OFFLINE') and write the exact query results—**including a
       one-line CSV header**—to the file  
       /home/user/iot_edge/offline_devices.log

       The file MUST contain precisely the following two lines and nothing else:  
           device_id,hostname,status  
           2,edge-node-02,OFFLINE  

    A quick checklist for you:  
       • Directory path:  /home/user/iot_edge  
       • SQLite file:     /home/user/iot_edge/iot_devices.db  
       • Log file:        /home/user/iot_edge/offline_devices.log  

    The automated grader will verify:  
       a. correct directory and file names;  
       b. table schema and row contents;  
       c. exact two-line CSV in offline_devices.log.  

    Keep the number of commands small (about three or four).  
    Do NOT display the entire database—only create it, insert the rows, and
    generate the log file in the required format.
truth:
Final directory structure:
        /home/user/iot_edge/
            ├── iot_devices.db      (SQLite database)
            └── offline_devices.log (CSV text file)

    Required SQLite contents:
        Database: /home/user/iot_edge/iot_devices.db
        Tables: device_info

        Schema of device_info (as returned by PRAGMA table_info(device_info);):
            cid | name       | type      | notnull | dflt_value | pk
            ----+------------+-----------+---------+------------+---
            0   | device_id  | INTEGER   | 0       | NULL       | 1
            1   | hostname   | TEXT      | 1       | NULL       | 0
            2   | ip_address | TEXT      | 1       | NULL       | 0
            3   | status     | TEXT      | 1       | NULL       | 0
            4   | last_seen  | TIMESTAMP | 1       | NULL       | 0

        Rows of device_info (as returned by SELECT * FROM device_info ORDER BY device_id;):
            1|edge-node-01|192.168.10.21|ONLINE |2023-08-18 10:40:00
            2|edge-node-02|192.168.10.22|OFFLINE|2023-08-18 09:20:00
            3|sensor-hub-01|192.168.10.31|ONLINE |2023-08-18 10:35:00

    Required log file: /home/user/iot_edge/offline_devices.log  
        Exact file content (including final newline at end of file):
            device_id,hostname,status
            2,edge-node-02,OFFLINE

        The file must contain exactly two lines—one header and one data line—and must
        end with a single trailing newline (i.e., the file is 48 bytes long).

    Permissions: default user-level permissions are sufficient (no root needed).
name: task_000000_24a43159
